spec_match.rationale: Showcases the real-world payoff of the spec: setAttribute / getAttribute / dataset all accept Unicode data-* names (Japanese, Spanish, Russian, Arabic) — exactly what the relaxation unlocks for i18n authoring.

interactive.rationale: Native-support probe, four-language catalog with Read-attributes button, dataset camelCase mapping table, and a live setAttribute + dataset round-trip tester — all real DOM calls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'data-名前, data-precio, data-название' with dataset camelCase table and live setter/reader — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Dataset table covers the hyphen → camelCase conversion well; but never demonstrates the RTL bi-di pitfall when the Arabic key name renders inside the table, nor the case-folding asymmetry between attribute names (lowercased) and dataset keys (preserved). Also: try/catch swallows errors silently in the catalog build, which hides any setAttribute throw from a visitor on pre-v143.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/6278918763708416 and the DOM namespaces spec.

design_system.rationale: Most colours from variables, but `.result-box.ok { background: #edfdf0 }` and `.result-box.err { background: #fde8e8 }` are raw hex backgrounds — outside the documented --bg / --accent / --text token set. Replace with --accent-emerald / --accent-rose (or token-derived tints) so the page stays in the design system.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex result-box backgrounds with design-system tokens
openQuestions.detail: `#edfdf0` and `#fde8e8` violate the no-raw-hex rule. Use `var(--accent-emerald)` and `var(--accent-rose)` directly (and check contrast against --text-black) or define small tint tokens in styles.css if a softer wash is intentional.

openQuestions.title: Show the silent-failure modes
openQuestions.detail: Catalog construction wraps each setAttribute in try/catch with no fallback message. On pre-v143 every Unicode attribute silently disappears and the catalog still renders. Either surface a 'N attributes dropped' note when the support probe is red, or remove the try/catch so the real spec behaviour is visible.

openQuestions.title: Cover the dataset case-preservation edge case
openQuestions.detail: The mapping table shows hyphen → camelCase; worth a separate row that highlights `data-名前-A` → `dataset['名前A']` and contrasts with `data-foo-A` → `dataset.fooA`. Unicode characters are not case-folded, ASCII letters keep their case in the key — both spec behaviours worth surfacing explicitly.

summary: Rich real-world demo of i18n data-* attributes. The required ChromeStatus and DOM spec links are now present; remaining follow-ups are design-token cleanup and a few edge-case visibility improvements.
